revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24608848913|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Atago Shrine]][[fr:Atago-jinja]][[ja:愛宕神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ko:아타고 신사 (동음이의)]][[sv:Atago-jinja]]
An Atago Shrrine is a shrine of the {{ill|Atago Faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Faith|qq=}} {{Nihongo|'''Atago Shrine'''|愛宕神社|Atago Jinja|qq=}} may refer to: * [[Atago Shrine (Kyoto)]] * [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)]] * Atago Shrine, once located on [[Atagoyama Kofun]], Gyōdo, Saitama Prefecture * Atago Shrine, on [[Atagoyama Kofun (Mito)]], Ibaraki Prefecture ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Atago Shrine |Alias = Atago Sha |Description = Shrines named "Atago." |Summary = Atago Shrines number approximately 900 nationwide, with the head shrine located in Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. Formerly dedicated to the syncretic deity Atago Gongen, most now venerate {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} and {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} decree. |MainShrine = [[Atago Shrine (Kyoto City)|Atago Shrines]] (Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture) }} '''Atago Shrine''' (愛宕神社 or 愛宕社, Atago Jinja/Atago Sha) is the name for a number of Shinto shrines across Japan. See also for more information. == Head Shrine == * '''{{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|愛宕神社 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture) == Hokkaido == * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Oginishi, Kaminokuni)|ja|愛宕神社 (上ノ国町字扇石)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Oginishi, Kaminokuni)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502361|1=Atago Shrine (Oginishi, Kaminokuni)}} (Kaminokuni Town, Hiyama District) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Katsuraoka, Kaminokuni)|ja|愛宕神社 (上ノ国町字桂岡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Katsuraoka, Kaminokuni)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502363|1=Atago Shrine (Katsuraoka, Kaminokuni)}} (Kaminokuni Town, Hiyama District) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Esashi Town)|ja|愛宕神社 (江差町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Esashi Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502364|1=Atago Shrine (Esashi Town)}} (Esashi Town, Hiyama District) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Shiraoi Town)|ja|愛宕神社 (白老郡白老町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Shiraoi Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502366|1=Atago Shrine (Shiraoi Town)}} (Shiraoi Town, Shiraoi District) - Dedicated from {{ill|Atago Shrine (Sendai)|en|Atago Shrine (Sendai)|qid=Q11493856|lt=Atago Shrine (Sendai)|1=Atago Shrine (Sendai)}}. == Tohoku Region == * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Oshu)|ja|愛宕神社 (奥州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Oshu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502368|1=Atago Shrine (Oshu)}} (Ōshū City, Iwate Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Sendai)|en|Atago Shrine (Sendai)|qid=Q11493856|lt=Atago Shrine (Sendai)|1=Atago Shrine (Sendai)}} (Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Matsushima)|ja|愛宕神社 (松島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Matsushima)|qq=}} (Matsushima Town, Miyagi Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Toyosato, Tome)|ja|愛宕神社 (登米市豊里町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Toyosato, Tome)|qq=}} (Tome City, Miyagi Prefecture) == Kanto Region == * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Mito)|ja|愛宕神社 (水戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Mito)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502371|1=Atago Shrine (Mito)}} (Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Tsuchiura)|ja|愛宕神社 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Tsuchiura)|qq=}} (Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Tamagawa, Naka)|ja|愛宕神社 (行方市玉造甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Tamagawa, Naka)|1=Atago Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493867}} (Naka City, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Koga)|ja|愛宕神社 (久喜市久喜中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Koga)|1=Atago Shrine (Kuki Honcho)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493855}} (Kuki City, Saitama Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|en|Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|qid=Q2868748|lt=Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|1=Atago Shrine (Tokyo)}} (Minato City, Tokyo) == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Komachi Atago Shrine|ja|古町愛宕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komachi Atago Shrine|1=Komachi Atago Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411527}} (Niigata City, Chuo Ward, Niigata Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Toyama)|ja|愛宕神社 (富山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Toyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502372|1=Atago Shrine (Toyama)}} (Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Gujō)|ja|愛宕神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Gujō)|qq=}} (Gujo City, Gifu Prefecture) == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Koka)|ja|愛宕神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Koka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502374|1=Atago Shrine (Koka)}} (Kōka City, Shiga Prefecture) - Also known as "Pottery Shrine." * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto, Sakyo)|ja|愛宕神社 (京都市左京区)|1=Kino Atago Shrine|2=ja|3=愛宕神社 (京都市左京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Kyoto, Sakyo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48762420}} (Sakyo Ward, Kyoto City) == Kyushu Region == * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kitakyushu)|ja|愛宕神社 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Kitakyushu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502375|1=Atago Shrine (Kitakyushu)}} (Kitakyushu City, Fukuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Washio Atago Shrine|ja|鷲尾愛宕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washio Atago Shrine|qq=}} (Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture)
== See Also == * {{ill|Atago Faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Faith|qq=}} * {{ill|Atago Matsuri|ja|愛宕祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Festival|qq=}} {{Disambiguation|qq=}} ==See also== * [[Atago Gongen]] {{Disambiguation|qq=}}
{{Atago Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|愛宕神社 (曖昧さ回避)|version=103665136|comment=Imported full ja history}}
